rulings.Case Title: RAMESH SHRESTHA VS. HOTEL SUNRISE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANTs HOTEL SUNRISE, INC., HOTEL SUNRISE, LLC, KIRITKUMAR PATEL, SHAKUNTLA PATEL, and KETAN PATEL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to Defendant Hotel Sunrise, LLC. Non-opposition filed. Motion is GRANTED as to defendant Ketan Patel. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact as to Ketal Patel not being Plaintiffs employer/landlord/licensor. Motion is GRANTED as to causes of action for IIED and NIED. Moving party shifted its burden to show that Defendants' conduct is not "extreme and outrageous" within the meaning of these causes of action; Plaintiff failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. Motion is denied as to causes of action arising out of residential occupancy of the premises by the Plaintiffs. Triable fact exists as to whether Plaintiffs were tenants or licensees. Motion is DENIED as to Defendants Ken and Shakuntla Patel re: causes of action arising out of Plaintiff's employment. Triable issue of fact exists as to whether Defendants were joint employers with the corporate defendant. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MARZO ET AL VS. CORRESE DI GRAZIA ARTIERES ET AL

rulings.Case Title: HARRISON H. KUI ET AL VS. NU TO THANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANTs NU THANG, EKARIO THANG, and HUE TRINH's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice is compliant with Ellis Act re: extension rights, disability-related rights. Owner complied with SFRO 37.9A(f)(4)(E). Further, Ellis Act does not require this 5-year reporting (SFRO 37.9A(h)) for the purposes of going out of residential rental market. To the extent SFRO attempts to create an additional requirement for the landlord to accomplish the withdrawal, SFRO is pre-empted. To the extent SFRO is exercising police power to regulate the subsequent use of the property after the withdrawal, no defense to the UD is created. = (501/REQ)
